title: 802.1X Supplicant Timeout Tracking
dataSources: RADIUS server logs (FreeRADIUS, NPS, ISE), switch/WLC syslog (dot1x events)
app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, `Splunk_TA_windows` (NPS), FreeRADIUS syslog, `TA-cisco_ios`, HPE Aruba CX syslog, RADIUS TA, NAS syslog
spl:
index=nac (sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth" OR sourcetype="radius:auth" OR sourcetype="freeradius")
| search "timeout" OR "timed out" OR "EAP timeout" OR "supplicant" OR "no response"
| rex field=_raw "Calling-Station-Id=(?<mac>[^\s]+)"
| rex field=_raw "NAS-Identifier=(?<nas>[^\s,]+)"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count by mac, nas, _time
| where count > 3
| sort -count
description: Clients timing out during RADIUS authentication (common issue with Wi-Fi and wired NAC). Tracking timeouts helps identify supplicant misconfiguration, certificate issues, or network latency affecting authentication.
value: Clients timing out during RADIUS authentication (common issue with Wi-Fi and wired NAC). Tracking timeouts helps identify supplicant misconfiguration, certificate issues, or network latency affecting authentication.
implementation: Forward RADIUS authentication logs and NAS (switch/WLC) syslog to Splunk. Configure sourcetypes for FreeRADIUS, NPS, or ISE. Extract Calling-Station-Id (MAC), NAS-Identifier, and timeout-related messages. Alert when timeout count exceeds 5 per NAS per hour. Correlate with switch port and SSID to identify problematic segments. Report on timeout trends by location and time of day.
visualization: Line chart (timeout rate over time), Bar chart (timeouts by NAS/switch), Table (affected MACs and NAS), Heatmap (NAS × hour of day).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Authentication pattern spikes during ISE or RADIUS maintenance, certificate renewal windows, or RADIUS time-outs that affect many clients at once.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, `Splunk_TA_windows` (NPS), FreeRADIUS syslog, `TA-cisco_ios`, HPE Aruba CX syslog, RADIUS TA, NAS syslog.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: RADIUS server logs (FreeRADIUS, NPS, ISE), switch/WLC syslog (dot1x events).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Forward RADIUS authentication logs and NAS (switch/WLC) syslog to Splunk. Configure sourcetypes for FreeRADIUS, NPS, or ISE. Extract Calling-Station-Id (MAC), NAS-Identifier, and timeout-related messages. Alert when timeout count exceeds 5 per NAS per hour. Correlate with switch port and SSID to identify problematic segments. Report on timeout trends by location and time of day.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nac (sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth" OR sourcetype="radius:auth" OR sourcetype="freeradius")
| search "timeout" OR "timed out" OR "EAP timeout" OR "supplicant" OR "no response"
| rex field=_raw "Calling-Station-Id=(?<mac>[^\s]+)"
| rex field=_raw "NAS-Identifier=(?<nas>[^\s,]+)"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count by mac, nas, _time
| where count > 3
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**802.1X Supplicant Timeout Tracking** — Clients timing out during RADIUS authentication (common issue with Wi-Fi and wired NAC). Tracking timeouts helps identify supplicant misconfiguration, certificate issues, or network latency affecting authentication.

Documented **Data sources**: RADIUS server logs (FreeRADIUS, NPS, ISE), switch/WLC syslog (dot1x events). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, `Splunk_TA_windows` (NPS), FreeRADIUS syslog, `TA-cisco_ios`, HPE Aruba CX syslog, RADIUS TA, NAS syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nac; **sourcetype**: cisco:ise:auth, radius:auth, freeradius. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nac, sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Extracts fields with `rex` (regular expression).
• Extracts fields with `rex` (regular expression).
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by mac, nas, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where count > 3` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco ISE Live Log and endpoint details for the same sessions, MACs, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (timeout rate over time), Bar chart (timeouts by NAS/switch), Table (affected MACs and NAS), Heatmap (NAS × hour of day).
grandmaExplanation: We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.
hardware: Cisco ISE, Windows NPS, FreeRADIUS, Cisco/Aruba switches and WLCs
